info.name: Uniblox E-Commerce API
info.description:
Happy path flow:
1. Browse products
2. Add items to cart
3. Preview checkout (no side effects)
4. Checkout (repeat Nth times to hit milestone)
5. Coupon is AUTO-generated after Nth checkout — check GET /api/admin/coupons
6. Checkout with coupon code
7. View your order history
8. Check admin stats

Base URL: http://localhost:3000
Run `npm run dev` before testing.

Set the `couponCode` collection variable after a coupon appears in List All Coupons.

request.description: Returns all 5 seeded products with current stock levels. Use product IDs (p1–p5) in cart requests.

Adds Wireless Headphones (p1) × 2 to the cart. Price is snapshotted at add-time.

ADD semantics — if p1 is already in the cart, quantity accumulates. Use PUT to set an absolute quantity.
Adds USB-C Hub (p3) × 1. Cart now has 2 items.
Returns cart contents with running total.

Sets the quantity of p1 to an absolute value (SET semantics — not additive).

Use this to reduce a quantity after stock has dropped. Stock check validates against the new absolute quantity, not the delta.

Pass quantity: 0 to remove the item without calling DELETE.
Removes USB-C Hub (p3) from the cart entirely. Returns the updated cart.
Expected: 400 InsufficientStockError. Webcam HD (p4) has only 20 units.

Runs all checkout validations and returns the price breakdown — without placing an order or consuming the coupon.

The coupon's redeemedBy list is NOT updated. Use this to show the user their discounted total before confirming payment.

couponCode is optional — leave blank for a preview without discount.

Places an order. Stock is decremented, cart cleared.

Response includes nextCouponAt — orders remaining until the next coupon milestone.

When nextCouponAt reaches 0, a USER_SPECIFIC coupon is AUTO-GENERATED by the event handler and will appear in GET /api/admin/coupons. No admin action needed.

Checkout with a discount coupon.

- USER_SPECIFIC: only redeemable by the user it was issued to; single-use globally.
- GLOBAL: any user can redeem it, but each user can only use it once.

Expected: discount applied, userId added to coupon's redeemedBy list.
Expected: 404 CartNotFoundError. user2 has no cart.

Run after 'Checkout With Coupon'.

- USER_SPECIFIC: Expected 400 CouponAlreadyUsedError (globally spent).
- GLOBAL: Expected 400 — you have already used this coupon.

Returns all orders placed by the user, sorted newest-first.

Includes totalOrders and totalSpend summary.

This is the user-facing endpoint — distinct from GET /api/admin/stats/:userId which is admin-scoped and mixes aggregate stats with order data.

Returns 404 if the user has not placed any orders yet.

Manual override only — USER_SPECIFIC coupons are AUTO-GENERATED by the checkout event handler on every Nth order. You do NOT need to call this after a successful checkout.

Use this endpoint only to test the milestone logic manually or as a fallback if the event handler failed.

Returns success:false if user hasn't hit the milestone — valid business state, not an error.

GLOBAL coupons are always manual — admin decides when to run a campaign.

TIER1: 7.5% off on orders above ₹1500. Any user can redeem it, but each user can only use it once (tracked via redeemedBy[]).
GLOBAL TIER2: 10% off on orders above ₹2000. Higher discount, higher minimum threshold.

Lists every coupon — USER_SPECIFIC and GLOBAL.

The `redeemedBy` field shows which user IDs have consumed each coupon:
- Empty array [] = unused
- USER_SPECIFIC: max 1 entry (globally single-use)
- GLOBAL: one entry per user who has redeemed it (single-use per user)

After every Nth checkout, a new USER_SPECIFIC coupon will appear here automatically.

Returns aggregate store metrics:
- totalOrders, totalItemsPurchased, totalRevenue, totalDiscountGiven
- topSellingProduct: product with the highest total units sold
- topRevenueProduct: product that generated the most revenue
- All coupons with redemption status

Per-user stats scoped to user1 (admin view):
- totalOrders, totalItemsPurchased, totalRevenue, totalDiscountGiven
- favoriteProduct: the product user1 has purchased most
- orders[]: full order history with itemised details
- coupons: coupons issued to or redeemed by user1

For a user-facing order list (without admin stats), use GET /api/orders/:userId instead.
Returns server status and timestamp. Useful for readiness probes.
